findings.summary: Scaffold input can falsely satisfy source comparison.
findings.resolution: SOURCE_CORPUS binds primary IDs to hashed text or explicit attestation; scaffold paths are rejected.

findings.summary: Remote media can bypass provenance.
findings.resolution: Markdown and HTML remote media are release-blocking; local assets require structured provenance.

findings.summary: General rights evidence and license text are only weakly asserted.
findings.resolution: Hashed local evidence snapshots and exact official legal-text hashes are required.

findings.summary: Content and component index can be replaced together.
findings.resolution: An external accepted-state ledger anchors the accepted component-index hash.

findings.summary: Some accessibility-floor findings do not block release.
findings.resolution: Alt text, diagram/chart text alternatives, heading starts/jumps/empties, and descriptive links are release-blocking; post-render behavior remains explicitly human-reviewed.

findings.summary: Deliverable format checks are incomplete.
findings.resolution: Chapter skeleton, objective propagation, slide templates, assessment sections, and practice counts are validated.

findings.summary: Regex syntax checks miss plugins and invalid nesting.
findings.resolution: The real parser runs and generic plugin balance plus nested fence direction are enforced.

findings.summary: Paged source routing conflicts with the package contract.
findings.resolution: DOCUMENTS.json declares carrier type for sources in valid public/private/personal paths.

findings.summary: Pack does not require carrier-build and evaluation receipts.
findings.resolution: Packing reruns carriers and emits evaluation, archive hash, and structured release receipt.

findings.summary: Provenance, usedIn, and attribution are not fully cross-validated.
findings.resolution: Paths and usedIn are required, duplicates/orphans fail, and attribution is generated exactly.

findings.summary: An AI-use notice can be overlooked or incorrectly presented as an amendment to a Creative Commons copyright license.
findings.resolution: Capture preserves the exact notice, marks its legal effect undetermined, leaves non-published use open, and presents it as a nonblocking warning for an instructor considering publication.

findings.summary: Static carrier shells report a misleading zero rendered-image accessibility count.
findings.resolution: Receipts now label source and static-shell inspections precisely and require a separate runtime visual review instead of claiming rendered coverage.

findings.summary: Media-bearing carriers are described as self-contained although local assets remain external.
findings.resolution: Receipts classify single-file versus carrier-plus-assets modes, expose dependencies, and support strict --require-single-file enforcement; product wording was corrected.

findings.summary: Runtime Mermaid/chart meaning can disappear offline without a visible alternative.
findings.resolution: Public QA requires a substantive adjacent Visual description or Data summary for every Mermaid/chart block.

findings.summary: Public output and original assets can identify an AI agent instead of an accountable rights holder.
findings.resolution: Public foundations require a named non-AI author/rightsholder and self-generated/user-owned creators must match an accountable author.

findings.summary: A chapter can silently omit the real-photo expectation in Coursewerk authoring guidance.
findings.resolution: Each chapter either includes a rights-cleared photo or records a substantive accountable not-used decision in MEDIA_PLAN.json.

findings.summary: The carrier receipt hash cannot be reproduced because the exact receipt is not delivered.
findings.resolution: Packing persists and hash-binds the canonical .carriers.json beside the release receipt.

findings.summary: Extracted source text is hashed but the raw source and retrieval context are not digest-bound.
findings.resolution: Automatic corpus entries include raw snapshot path/hash, canonical URL, retrieval timestamp, versioned extractor metadata, extracted-text hash, and an exact manifest hash in the release receipt.

findings.summary: A known public source can claim verified evidence when its expected notice is absent.
findings.resolution: Known-source evidence expectations are enforced during capture and rechecked from a hash-bound receipt before public preparation; non-published use remains advisory.

findings.summary: Source-rights readiness can conflate non-published authoring with public release.
findings.resolution: Non-published source use is advisory and never requires a receipt; public-source evidence and release claims remain separately verified, while AI-use notices do not become copyright conclusions.

findings.summary: Report commands fail when the report parent directory does not exist.
findings.resolution: Assurance and QA commands create report/JSON parent directories before writing.

findings.summary: Rights snapshots omit retrieval and capture provenance needed to interpret the evidence.
findings.resolution: Capture records mode, URLs/redirects, HTTP metadata, bytes, timestamp, tool version, and structured operator identity.

findings.summary: Detected process-notice excerpts contain raw HTML fragments.
findings.resolution: Process scanning and excerpts operate on decoded visible text while preserving the raw snapshot separately.
